channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19503] quota soft limit is not working
item.description:
From the document "The grace period applies to all users. The user block soft limit is for all users who are using a blocks quota.
Soft limit – The grace timer is started once the soft limit is exceeded. At this point, the user/group/project
can still allocate block/inode. When the grace time expires and if the user is still above the soft limit, the
soft limit becomes a hard limit and the user/group/project can't allocate any new block/inode any more.
The user/group/project should then delete files to be under the soft limit. The soft limit MUST be smaller
than the hard limit. If the soft limit is not needed, it should be set to zero (0).
Hard limit – Block or inode allocation will fail with EDQUOT(i.e. quota exceeded) when the hard limit
is reached. The hard limit is the absolute limit. When a grace period is set, one can exceed the soft limit
within the grace period if under the hard limit.
"

below test can easily re-create the issue
item.summary: quota soft limit is not working

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62412/
Subject: LU-19503 doc: Clarify quota limit enforcement behavior
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c8d28267f9ed11a1cd851d3a50f1dc8b746162be
sorry did not see the comment due to browser issue. will fix it

Hi Rajeev, you should address the comments on the patch by submitting an updated patch. We require two reviewers for patches, and Sergey is more of a quota expert than I am, so if he marked it -1 it is for good reason.
I see the documentation changes have been rejected after approval. Please let me know if the doc changes are required or not

"Rajeev Mishra <rajeevm@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62412
Subject: LU-19503 doc: Clarify quota limit enforcement behavior
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: e4249c33ff3d292f84216f7dd6aa61a447ddab46
rajeevm, it might be worthwhile to update Documentation/man1/lfs-setquota.1 to document that the limits are not strictly enforced should preferably be in the GBs, as described above.

The problem with cached writes is that if the error is not generated at the instant the write() call is complete, then the application will often ignore it when there is an opportunity to return an error from fsync() (rarely called) or close() (ignored, because it is too late to do anything about it). So, rather than failing data writes that exceed quota and losing user data, Lustre prefers to allow those cached writes to succeed over quota, and only prevent future writes.

On the one hand, "lazy quota" bugs administrators who want users to only use X GB/TB of storage (honestly, considering MB units of quota is almost meaningless today when storage is cents per GB), but on the other hand it is the user data that is most important in the end, so it is better to write out that cached data and use up some small amount of free space in the filesystem rather than having to re-run hours of compute workload. With many clients and large caches, the cached data might still be GB, but the filesystem will be PB in size at that point so it is still a reasonable tradeoff.

If there is something lacking, it is probably notification to users of soft-quota exceeded. This was at one time implemented with a D_TTY debug message that hooked into the tty and wrote directly to the stdout of the application, but I wasn't a fan of that at the time. Maybe it is time to change my mind, if this would make it better for systems to have a soft quota limit that users could actually be notified about. Alternately, if the Lustre quota could somehow be hooked into the normal kernel quota tools so that they print a message when the user logs in, that might also be a usable solution.

Thanks, @Andreas. Your suggestion helped (as always). The new script works — once it started writing more data, the error appeared as expected.

I’m still going through the code to understand it better. I can see that osc_quota_chkdq returns EDQUOT, but somehow the write still proceeds. I am a bit confused — when a cached write encounters EDQUOT, why doesn’t it fail immediately?

With the direct flag, the failure now occurs as expected since lqe_edquot is set, similar to the hard limit path. The caching side seems quite complex, I will increase the size of write and check if we ever fail with caching on. Any input ? if cached write will fail with soft limit Thanks again

# Enable quota enforcement for users and groups
sudo chown rocky:rocky /mnt/lustre
sudo rm -fr /mnt/lustre/*
sudo lctl set_param osc.*.max_dirty_mb=1
sudo lctl conf_param lustre.quota.ost=ugp
sudo lctl conf_param lustre.quota.mdt=ugp

# Or try enabling directly on each target
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-MDT0000.quota_slave.enabled=ugp
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-OST0000.quota_slave.enabled=ugp
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-OST0001.quota_slave.enabled=ugp

# Verify quota is enabled
sudo lctl get_param osd-ldiskfs.*.quota_slave.info | grep "quota enabled"

# Set soft limit = 4MB, no hard limit initially
sudo lfs setquota -u rocky -b 4096 -B 0 -i 0 -I 0 /mnt/lustre

# Optional: mirror the same quota for the group
sudo lfs setquota -g rocky -b 4096 -B 0 -i 0 -I 0 /mnt/lustre

# Check quota
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

# Set grace time (20s for blocks, 300s for inodes)
sudo lfs setquota -t -u --block-grace 20 --inode-grace 300 /mnt/lustre
sudo lfs setquota -t -g --block-grace 20 --inode-grace 300 /mnt/lustre

# Verify grace settings
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre
sudo lfs quota -t /mnt/lustre

# Write 4MB to reach soft limit
echo "write 4MB"
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=4 oflag=direct

sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
# Check quota usage (should be at limit)
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre
echo "Write beyond 4096"
# Write beyond soft limit during grace period (should succeed)
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl set_param -n debug="+quota+trace"
sudo lctl set_param debug_mb=512
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl set_param debug=-1
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl clear 

sudo /usr/sbin/lctl mark "Rajeev write to exceed soft"
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=4 seek=4096 oflag=direct
sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

# sync  
#sudo lctl set_param -t4 -n "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lru_size=clear"
#sudo lctl get_param "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lock_unused_count" | grep -v '=0'
sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches

# Wait for grace to expire
sleep 25
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

sudo /usr/sbin/lctl mark "Rajeev write to exceed soft and grace time "
echo "Write beyond 4096 after grace"
# Try writing after grace expiration (soft limit exceeded, may fail or partially succeed)
#sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1K count=10 seek=8192
#sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1K count=10 oflag=append conv=notrunc
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=10 oflag=append,direct conv=notrunc
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl dk > /tmp/rajeev_soft 
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

#########################################
#  HARD LIMIT TEST
#########################################
echo "HARD QUOTA LIMIT"

# Now set a hard limit of 6MB (soft = 4MB, hard = 6MB)
sudo lfs setquota -u rocky -b 4096 -B 6144 -i 0 -I 0 /mnt/lustre
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

# Write additional 2MB to reach hard limit (should succeed)
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=2 seek=4
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

sudo /usr/sbin/lctl clear 
# sync 
#sudo lctl set_param -t4 -n "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lru_size=clear"
#sudo lctl get_param "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lock_unused_count" | grep -v '=0'
sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
# Attempt to write beyond hard limit (should fail with "Disk quota exceeded")
#sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=1 seek=6 || echo "Write beyond hard limit failed as expected"
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl  mark "Rajeev write to exceed hard limit "
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=1 seek=6 
sudo /usr/sbin/lctl  dk > /tmp/rajeev_hard

sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

Rajeev, I can't comment about the specific details about the issue here, but in general testing with quota limits of only a few MB is going to be difficult to get accurate behavior, since this still falls into the "grey area" of client-side writeback cache for the files. I would suggest to test this with larger files and quotas, and also reduce the osc.*.max_dirty_mb on the client and/or use "oflag=direct" to avoid client side cache.

#Enable quota enforcement for users and groups
sudo chown rocky:rocky /mnt/lustre
sudo rm -fr /mnt/lustre/*

sudo lctl conf_param lustre.quota.ost=ugp
sudo lctl conf_param lustre.quota.mdt=ugp

# try enabling directly on each target
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-MDT0000.quota_slave.enabled=ugp
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-OST0000.quota_slave.enabled=ugp
sudo lctl set_param osd-ldiskfs.lustre-OST0001.quota_slave.enabled=ugp

# Verify quota is enabled
sudo lctl get_param osd-ldiskfs.*.quota_slave.info | grep "quota enabled"

# Set soft limit = 4MB, no hard limit initially
sudo lfs setquota -u rocky -b 4096 -B 0 -i 0 -I 0 /mnt/lustre

# Optional: mirror the same quota for the group
sudo lfs setquota -g rocky -b 4096 -B 0 -i 0 -I 0 /mnt/lustre

# Check quota
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

# Set grace time (20s for blocks, 300s for inodes)
sudo lfs setquota -t -u --block-grace 20 --inode-grace 300 /mnt/lustre
sudo lfs setquota -t -g --block-grace 20 --inode-grace 300 /mnt/lustre

# Verify grace settings
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre
sudo lfs quota -t /mnt/lustre

# Write 4MB to reach soft limit
echo "write 4MB"
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=4

sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
# Check quota usage (should be at limit)
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre
echo "Write beyond 4096"
# Write beyond soft limit during grace period (should succeed)
sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1M count=4 seek=4096
sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

# sync  
#sudo lctl set_param -t4 -n "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lru_size=clear"
#sudo lctl get_param "ldlm.namespaces.*$1*.lock_unused_count" | grep -v '=0'
sudo sync                       # write dirty data to disk
echo 3 | sudo tee /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches

# Wait for grace to expire
sleep 25
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre

echo "Write beyond 4096 after grace"
# Try writing after grace expiration (soft limit exceeded, may fail or partially succeed)

sudo -u rocky dd if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/testfile bs=1K count=10 oflag=append conv=notrunc
sudo lfs quota -u rocky /mnt/lustre
